result:
The fix worked — the test now passes all 4 steps. No temporary eprintln!() were added, so nothing to clean up.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2355` in `constructor()`. The C code does `init_exp(t, VNONRELOC, fs->freereg); luaK_reserveregs(fs, 1);` but the Rust port left the `reserve_regs` call commented out as `// TODO(port): lua_code::reserve_regs(...)`. So the table constructor expression claimed `e.u.info = 0` (NonReloc, register 0) while `fs.freereg` stayed at 0. Later in `adjust_assign` → `cg_exp_to_next_reg` → `cg_free_exp` → `cg_free_reg(0)`, the LIFO assertion `debug_assert_eq!(reg, fs.freereg - 1)` fired with `0 != -1`.

**Fix:** Replaced the TODO comment with an actual call to the existing local helper: `reserve_regs(ls.fs.as_mut().unwrap(), 1)?;` (one line). The `reserve_regs` function was already defined at line 610.

**New error:** None — the full pipeline now succeeds: `[1/4]…[4/4] Executing chunk... [ok] execution completed, status=Ok`. The test program `local t = {10,20,30}; print(t[1])` runs cleanly end-to-end.
